stories.title: Free Thursday sessions in Shotton and Buckley funded by £48,000 UK Government grant
stories.summary: A free weekly darts club in Shotton and a 60+ exercise programme at a Buckley care home are among the activities running under a county-wide scheme funded by the UK Government Shared Prosperity Fund. Actif North Wales received £48,000 from Flintshire County Council’s allocation of the fund for the 2025-26 continuation year to deliver the

stories.title: £400 bonus on offer as Coleg Cambria targets Welsh-speaking talent
stories.summary: Welsh speakers are invited to explore new career opportunities with Coleg Cambria at a dedicated bilingual recruitment event – and there is a £400 welcome bonus available for eligible new starters. Taking place on Wednesday April 29 at the college’s Yale site in Wrexham, Gyrfa Gymraeg @ Cambria will offer a relaxed and informal opportunity

stories.title: Deadline to register for Senedd election is Monday — hundreds of thousands not yet signed up
stories.summary: Voters in Flintshire have until 11.59pm on Monday 20 April to register to vote in the Senedd election, as campaigners warn that around 400,000 people across Wales are either missing from the electoral register or incorrectly registered. The figures come from the Electoral Commission and have been highlighted by the Electoral Reform Society (ERS) Cymru,

stories.title: Police appeal after driver fails to stop following collision with cyclist in Buckley
stories.summary: North Wales Police are appealing for witnesses after a silver vehicle struck a cyclist on Liverpool Road in Buckley and failed to stop. The collision was reported at 10.01am on Thursday. Officers from South Flintshire police are asking anyone who witnessed the incident, or who has dashcam footage from the area around that time, to

stories.title: Eight nations to compete in north Wales as UEFA Under-19 Championship draw takes place
stories.summary: The draw for this summer’s UEFA European Under-19 Championship in north Wales has taken place at Wrexham University, confirming the eight nations competing in the tournament. Croatia, Denmark, Germany, Italy, Serbia, Spain and Ukraine will join hosts Wales across four venues from 28 June to 11 July. Games will be played at the Racecourse Ground
